_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how to use our knowledge of sounds and word parts to read and write multi-syllable words with complex letter combinations.

_source.w: We are learning how to use our knowledge of sounds and word parts to read and write multi-syllable words with complex letter combinations.
y: Tackling multi-syllable words confidently opens up more challenging and interesting texts.
Combining phonological and morphological knowledge gives us multiple strategies for decoding.
These skills are essential for the increasingly complex reading we do across all subjects.
eg: We break complex words into syllables and morphemes to decode them — like 'un-for-get-table'.
We use our knowledge of common letter combinations to read words with unusual spelling patterns.
We apply phonological knowledge to spell long words by working through them syllable by syllable.
We practise reading multi-syllable words fluently so they do not slow us down.
We identify the base word in complex words and use it to figure out meaning.
We keep a personal list of challenging multi-syllable words we have learned to decode.
